Q32: My first exposure to marine science was digitally and I didn't get any exposure to the Marine environment until I studied marine science in college. I knew it was something I always wanted to study but I didn't have the resources to do anything as a kid. I'm so thankful to have studied marine science and to now have jobs where I can continue doing research and science communications and education/outreach. Im a woman, Latina, and indigenous and we need more people that look like me in theses spaces. It was so hard for me to feel confident as a scientist but I'm thankful for my family, college support programs, and some professors that kept me motivated and empowered me. I make it my mission to help encourage more coastal communities of color and even minorities that don't live near the ocean to take up space and feel like they belong in coastal areas and can even be scientists. I'm hoping for more support for other minorities in stem to keep providing these opportunities to others. I'm very thankful for this survey and I really hope that change happens! There are some many of us that want to improve our communities and we want to be involved - please invite us and give us space. Thank you so much!
